faqs.question: What's the difference between hornets, yellow jackets, and wasps?
faqs.answer: Hornets are larger than yellow jackets and build aerial paper nests, often in trees or under eaves. Yellow jackets typically nest in ground cavities or wall voids and are more aggressive. Wasps generally build smaller, open-comb nests. All three require professional removal in Carle Place, but identification helps determine the safest removal approach.
